codeReview.summary: Reviewed the mission docs, fix handoff, transcript skeleton, prior failed review, the original bot-attach-detach commit cc4f056780c0bf333f2484849d761dbddf3716c7, and the fix commit 87c1e9994dd36dc85223172f3c75349f76ba6994. The fix replaces lossy `/sessions/:id/history` snapshot polling with a cursor-based per-user `/users/:id/events` feed, seeds the bot forwarder from the attach response's `userEventCursor`, and adds focused server, bot, and integration tests that reproduce bounded-history eviction while confirming all attached burst messages still reach Feishu. I did not find any new blocking correctness issues in the reviewed feature.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Shared architecture docs still describe attached output forwarding only at a high level and do not record the concrete per-user event feed contract introduced by this fix (`/users/:id/events` plus attach-time `userEventCursor`). Future workers would likely have to rediscover that API surface from code.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: .factory/library/architecture.md:67-72,79-80,102-113 still describes generic REST/polling forwarding; the reviewed implementation now depends on server/src/relay-server.ts:194-208,320-323 plus bot/src/relay.ts:149-167 and bot/src/bot-service.ts:563-647 in commit 87c1e9994dd36dc85223172f3c75349f76ba6994.

summary: Pass. The original blocking issue from bot-attach-detach is addressed: attached forwarding no longer depends on bounded history snapshots, and the new regression tests specifically prove bursty agent output is delivered losslessly to the attached bot user.
